title: Trust the timing, a breath practice
subtitle: A short meditation for releasing the pressure to force or rush an opportunity that hasn't arrived yet.
excerpt: A five-minute breath practice from Day 48 of the MONEY program. For when you are between moments, the opportunity is real but the timing is not yours to control.
sourceNotes: Inspired by Day 48 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Breath-anchored waiting practice draws on mindfulness traditions and somatic regulation approaches in the public domain.

content.text: A five-minute breath practice from Day 48 of the MONEY program. Waiting on big opportunities is one of the harder places to stay steady. The urge to push, check, or redirect comes from a body that has learned urgency. This practice gives the body something else to do with that energy.
Sit somewhere quiet, feet flat on the floor. Close your eyes or soften your gaze downward. You are not doing anything with this moment except breathing through it.
Breathe in for a count of four. Hold for four. Out for six. Repeat this pattern three times without naming anything. Just the numbers, just the breath.
On the fourth breath, bring to mind the thing you are waiting on. Not to solve it, just to name it. Let it sit in front of you, separate from you. It is on its way. You don't have to carry it.
Continue breathing. On each out breath, say to yourself: "I release the need to rush this." On each in breath: "I am ready when it arrives."
Do five rounds. Then let the words go and return to plain breath for one final minute.
Open your eyes. The opportunity is still there. The timing has not changed. But your relationship to the wait has, and that is the only thing that was ever yours to work with.
Inspired by Day 48 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Breath-anchored waiting practice draws on mindfulness traditions and somatic regulation approaches in the public domain.
